mnemonics.title: Painting Order
mnemonics.content: Very Polite Children Always Respect Parents (Validate → Paint Components → Add children → Repaint → Paint Borders). Remember Swing's painting sequence: 1) Validate 2) Paint Components 3) Paint Children 4) Paint Borders

mnemonics.title: PCUG Method
mnemonics.content: Override Paint Component Using Graphics: 'PCUG' reminds you to override paintComponent(Graphics g) method (not paint()) for custom drawing in Swing components

mnemonics.title: Repaint Triggers
mnemonics.content: CUP of Repaint: Components automatically repaint on 1) Component resize 2) User action 3) Programmatic repaint() call

mnemonics.title: Double Buffering
mnemonics.content: Smooth Art: Swing uses double buffering by default (like drawing on hidden canvas then flipping) to prevent flickering - imagine an artist's smooth sketchpad transition

acronyms.usage: Steps when overriding paintComponent: 1) Prepare by calling super.paintComponent(g) 2) Add custom drawing code 3) Inherit from JComponent 4) Never call paintComponent directly 5) Trigger repaints with repaint()
Custom painting rules: 1) Call superclass method 2) Code within paintComponent 3) Invoke repaint() to refresh 4) Never create Graphics object 5) Use Graphics parameter
Remember Swing components are lightweight vs AWT's heavyweight - affects painting mechanism and overlap handling

visualTips.visualization: Imagine components as layered transparent sheets - painting starts from bottom layer (component) up to top (borders)
Picture a whiteboard being wiped clean (erase background) before redrawing - that's what repaint() triggers
Visualize the Graphics parameter as an artist's toolbox containing brushes (drawRect), pens (drawLine), and paint cans (setColor)
Imagine two canvases - one being drawn secretly backstage (offscreen buffer) while audience sees the finished one (onscreen)

analogies.analogy: Like an artist's canvas - you're given a blank area (the component) and brushes (Graphics object) to create your masterpiece
A shared toolbox on a construction site - multiple components use the same Graphics object but can't modify it permanently
Like a window cleaner crew - they decide which windows (components) need cleaning (repainting) and in what order
Flipbook animation - you draw the next frame on the back page while viewers see the current front page, ensuring smooth transitions
